publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-24 18:21:17 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Three record books (1825-1839, 1839-1858, 1853-1874) which record regular covenant, special and annual meeting minutes and lists of members, male and female. The church had several revivals and passed resolutions against secret societies and for open seating.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: On December 11, 1824, twenty-four members of the Baptist Church of Reading were set off as the Baptist Society of Altay. The Society was officially constituted as a church on June 1, 1825. Until 1831 the church had no settled pastor and relied upon two licentiates. In 1842 the congregation built a church at a cost of $2,000. It was repaired and enlarged in 1861, and repaired again in 1874. In 1860 the church changed its name to the First Baptist Church of Altay to correspond to the post office and village. A parsonage was purchased in 1855. The church was a member of the Steuben Association from 1825 onward.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: This collection contains 3 record books (1825-1839, 1839-1858, 1853-1874) which record regular covenant, special and annual meeting minutes, and lists of male and female members. The church had several revivals (see below) and passed resolutions against secret societies and for open seating.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Baptist Church of Altay records, #6090. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Schuyler County (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: Secret societies -- Religious aspects -- Baptists.
Revivals -- New York (State) -- Altay.
Baptist women.
Churches, Baptist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of members continued from p. 9.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles of Faith and Practice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Covenant

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: December 11, 1824. Society is set off from the Baptist Church of Reading.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: January 1, 1825. First monthly covenant meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 1, 1825. The church is constituted by council.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The church plans to join the Steuben Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: January 1830: Resolution supporting the spread of the Gospel. May 1833: Many new members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: February 13, 1836: Brother is upset with "signs of the times sentiment. May 1838: Many new members. April 13, 1839: Discussion of the Sabbath controversy and the holding of a protracted meeting. July 13, 1839: Last meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of male members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of female members.

did.unittitle: A Book of Records of the Baptist Church of Christ at Tyrone,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: September - November 1844. Minutes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: September 1839: Minutes commence. March 6, 1847: Anti-secret society resolution. October 29, 1854: Discussion of various plans for donations to various benevolent societies. January 27, 1855: The church adopts the articles of faith of the Steuben Baptist Association. The articles of faith follow. December 27, 1856: A meeting to pray for a revival is held. January 30 - February 4, 1858: A revival.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of male members; names, if they are baptised, received or dismisses by letter, excluded, died or restored.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of female members with information as above.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: April 27, 1850. Trustee election meeting minutes, n.d. Trustee election meeting minutes.

did.unittitle: Records of the Tyrone Baptist Church,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Missing from the volume.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Covenant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of September 7, 1839, October 1839.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Records of the Tyrone Baptist Church"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes commence, April 24, 1858.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: August 25, 1860. The church decides to change its name to "The First Baptist Church of Altay" to correspond with the village and post office.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: December 1860. Revival.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: October 1862. Resolution on "free" seating in the church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May 27, 1865. A day of mourning for President Lincoln is declared.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: March 9, 1867. Many new members March 16, 1867. Many new members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Names of male members

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Names of female members. Articles of Faith adopted January 27, 1855.
